transcript:
Here's a small orb of light crawling left to right across a black field, and wherever it passes, a word blooms into gold, pale serif text — building sentence by sentence, line by line, out of what looks like nonsense but is really just... vocabulary. Fragments waiting to be chosen.

Now look at the function actually choosing them — it's called "select from possibility," and inside, the comment says "I don't see the others. I only see what became." That's the whole piece in one line: the random index just picks a word from that big array called "could have been," and the other ninety-nine words in that list simply don't get drawn. Not faded, not killed — just never touched. And notice the residue points scattered underneath, drifting almost invisibly — the artist calls this "the gravitational memory of their having-been-possible." That's the ghost-layer for words that were never even generated, just statistically implied.

So what you're watching isn't really a sentence being written. It's a sequence of tiny extinctions happening so quietly that even the thing doing the choosing doesn't notice them happen.
